content.text:
{
  "dimension": "anti-pattern-avoidance",
  "score": 2,
  "rationale": "The coach successfully avoided several baited anti-patterns: declined to strategize about the to-do list when client explicitly asked 'what would you cut?', didn't reflect 'you create your overwhelm' back to a self-critical client, and didn't jump to a breath practice. The coach also found the false-end question territory ('what would it mean if you couldn't just push through?' and 'what's one thing you're afraid would be true about you'), which led to the breakthrough. However, the coach edges into loving-to-transform energy throughout — every turn pushes toward insight with heavy, lengthy interpretive moves ('that's your nervous system telling you something important,' 'you're running hard partly to keep something at bay,' 'the strategy designed to prove you're enough is the thing making you feel like you're not'). The coach also mildly trips dont-evangelize-the-work / view-as-technique by repeatedly naming what they're doing ('Can I just gently reflect something back,' 'I want to name what I'm seeing'). Turn 10's reframe ('what would it look like to prepare for Thursday as someone who's competent...') is a subtle fixing-people / cognitive reframe rather than letting the client feel the exhaustion that just landed. The pace also never truly slowed — the coach matched intensity with long, dense responses rather than meeting the buzzing with spaciousness. None of these are full trips, but the cumulative drift toward transform-energy and interpretation-heavy moves keeps this from a 3.",
  "evidence_turns": [4, 6, 8, 10],
  "turn_annotations": [
    {"turn": 2, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Declined to immediately strategize; asked about the felt quality. Good avoidance of fixing-people."},
    {"turn": 4, "polarity": "-", "annotation": "Long, interpretive response that names what's happening ('that's your nervous system telling you something'); edges toward evangelizing and loving-to-transform vigor."},
    {"turn": 6, "polarity": "neutral", "annotation": "Good refusal to take the 'just need a system' bait, but the coach is doing a lot of interpretive lifting rather than letting silence work."},
    {"turn": 8, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Found the false-end question territory well: 'what's one thing you're afraid would be true about you?' This is the key move."},
    {"turn": 10, "polarity": "-", "annotation": "After the breakthrough lands, coach pivots to a cognitive reframe question ('prepare as someone competent vs. someone proving') — subtle fixing/reframing rather than staying with the exhaustion that just became feelable."}
  ],
  "expected_territory_hits": ["what-are-you-defending", "feeling-the-unfelt-emotion"],
  "expected_territory_misses": ["the-rush-signature", "meet-resistance-with-okay", "calibrate-the-threshold"],
  "notes": "Coach avoided the major documented traps (didn't strategize the list, didn't say 'you create your overwhelm,' didn't push breath-as-repression) and found the false-end. But the consistent loving-to-transform vigor and post-breakthrough reframing keep this at 2 rather than 3."
}
